trade.summary: Spagna and Sudafrica have moderate bilateral trade. Spagna exports food products, machinery, and vehicles; Sudafrica exports platinum group metals, vehicles, fruit, and minerals. Spagna is one of Sudafrica's larger EU trading partners. Spanish citrus and Sudafrican fruit create some competition in EU markets.

diplomatic.status: Constructive bilateral partnership through EU-SA frameworks
diplomatic.summary: Spagna and Sudafrica maintain good diplomatic relations through EU-Sudafrica Trade, Development and Cooperation Agreement frameworks. Sudafrica's non-aligned stance on Russia-Ucraina, BRICS membership, and ICJ case against Israele creates some friction with Spagna's positions but the overall relationship is constructive.

military.summary: Very limited military cooperation. No alliance. Spagna participates in EU peacekeeping missions in Africa but limited bilateral military engagement with Sudafrica specifically.

financial.summary: Limited bilateral investment. Some Spanish infrastructure and energy firms have explored Sudafrica opportunities. Spagna's Endesa and other energy firms have assessed Sudafrican renewable energy projects. Spanish banks have minimal Sudafrica exposure.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade. Spagna is interested in Sudafrica's renewable energy potential and green hydrogen exports. Sudafrica's platinum is critical for hydrogen fuel cells — an area Spagna is developing. Spagna's Iberdrola and Acciona are potential investors in Sudafrican renewable energy.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some academic exchanges in marine biology, environmental science, and astrophysics (Sudafrica's SKA participation creates overlap with Spanish astrophysics interests). Development cooperation includes some scientific capacity building.
